questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A stope yields 250 t of ore mixed with 25 t of waste. The percentage dilution is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dilution $=\dfrac{\text{waste}}{\text{ore}+\text{waste}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{25}{275}\times100=9.09\%$.

**Answer.** $9.09\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A main fan develops a pressure of 2400 Pa while passing 38 m³/s of air. The air power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=\dfrac{P\times Q}{1000}$ kW ($P$ in Pa, $Q$ in m³/s).

**Working.** $\dfrac{2400\times38}{1000}=91.2$ kW.

**Answer.** $91.2$ kW.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A jackhammer drills at 0.35 m/min for 45 minutes of net drilling time. The total hole length advanced is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Advance $=$ penetration rate $\times$ time.

**Working.** $0.35\times45=15.75$ m.

**Answer.** $15.75$ m.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A stope yields 180 t of ore mixed with 20 t of waste. The percentage dilution is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dilution $=\dfrac{\text{waste}}{\text{ore}+\text{waste}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{20}{200}\times100=10\%$.

**Answer.** $10\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A shovel with a 7 m³ bucket works at a fill factor of 0.85 and a cycle time of 30 s. Its theoretical output is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Output $=\dfrac{\text{bucket}\times\text{fill factor}\times3600}{\text{cycle (s)}}$ m³/h.

**Working.** $\dfrac{7\times0.85\times3600}{30}=714$ m³/h.

**Answer.** $714$ m³/h.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.2 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 15 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=0.2\times 15^2=45$ Pa.

**Answer.** $45$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.6 m³/min into a roadway carrying 50 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.6}{50}\times100=1.2\%$.

**Answer.** $1.2\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 15 m² at a mean velocity of 3.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=3.5\times 15=52.5$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $52.5$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 6 m and height 4 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=1.5$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times1.5)=7.78$ MPa.

**Answer.** $7.78$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An ore block of 2500 m³ has bulk density 3 t/m³. Its tonnage is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tonnage $=$ volume $\times$ density.

**Working.** $2500\times3=7500$ t.

**Answer.** $7500$ t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A stope yields 300 t of ore mixed with 50 t of waste. The percentage dilution is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dilution $=\dfrac{\text{waste}}{\text{ore}+\text{waste}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{50}{350}\times100=14.29\%$.

**Answer.** $14.29\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine pump delivers 0.05 m³/s of water against a head of 180 m at 72% efficiency. The input power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=\dfrac{\rho g Q H}{\eta}$ with $\rho=1000$ kg/m³, $g=9.81$ m/s².

**Working.** $P=\dfrac{1000\times9.81\times0.05\times180}{0.72}\div1000=122.63$ kW.

**Answer.** $122.63$ kW.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A fan characteristic is $P=22-0.55Q^2$ and the mine resistance is $P=0.5\,Q^2$. The operating air quantity $Q$ (intersection) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Operating point: fan pressure = mine pressure.

**Working.** $22-0.55Q^2=0.5Q^2\Rightarrow Q^2=\dfrac{22}{1.05}\Rightarrow Q=4.58$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $4.58$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Settling (sedimentation) ponds at a mine are provided to:
options: Remove suspended solids before water is discharged
questions.solution: **Answer.** Settling ponds let suspended solids settle so discharged water meets effluent norms.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.4 m³/min into a roadway carrying 15 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.4}{15}\times100=2.67\%$.

**Answer.** $2.67\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An opencast blast block has burden 4 m, spacing 4 m, bench height 7 m and rock density 2.55 t/m³. If 44.8 kg of explosive is used, the powder factor (tonnes broken per kg) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** PF $=\dfrac{\text{rock broken (t)}}{\text{explosive (kg)}}$.

**Working.** Volume $=4\times4\times7=112$ m³; mass $=112\times2.55=285.6$ t; PF $=\dfrac{285.6}{44.8}=6.37$ t/kg.

**Answer.** $6.37$ t/kg.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 1 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 15 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=1\times 15^2=225$ Pa.

**Answer.** $225$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 10 m² at a mean velocity of 1.2 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=1.2\times 10=12$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $12$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An SDL (Side Discharge Loader) in underground coal mines is used to:
options: Gather and load broken coal onto cars/conveyor
questions.solution: **Answer.** The SDL gathers blasted coal and side-discharges it to tubs or a conveyor.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A deposit holds 50 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 5 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{50}{5}=10$ years.

**Answer.** $10$ years.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 6 m² at a mean velocity of 1.8 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=1.8\times 6=10.8$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $10.8$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine pump delivers 0.09 m³/s of water against a head of 110 m at 76% efficiency. The input power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=\dfrac{\rho g Q H}{\eta}$ with $\rho=1000$ kg/m³, $g=9.81$ m/s².

**Working.** $P=\dfrac{1000\times9.81\times0.09\times110}{0.76}\div1000=127.79$ kW.

**Answer.** $127.79$ kW.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 8 m² at a mean velocity of 1.2 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=1.2\times 8=9.6$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $9.6$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A concentrator treats 800 t of ore assaying 4% metal at 92% recovery. The mass of metal recovered is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Metal recovered $=$ feed $\times\dfrac{\text{grade}}{100}\times\dfrac{\text{recovery}}{100}$.

**Working.** $800\times0.04\times0.92=29.44$ t.

**Answer.** $29.44$ t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine pump delivers 0.08 m³/s of water against a head of 150 m at 80% efficiency. The input power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=\dfrac{\rho g Q H}{\eta}$ with $\rho=1000$ kg/m³, $g=9.81$ m/s².

**Working.** $P=\dfrac{1000\times9.81\times0.08\times150}{0.8}\div1000=147.15$ kW.

**Answer.** $147.15$ kW.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 8 m and height 4 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=2$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times2)=8.55$ MPa.

**Answer.** $8.55$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A deposit holds 12 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 1.5 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{12}{1.5}=8$ years.

**Answer.** $8$ years.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.2 m³/min into a roadway carrying 40 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.2}{40}\times100=0.5\%$.

**Answer.** $0.5\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 6 m² at a mean velocity of 1.2 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=1.2\times 6=7.2$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $7.2$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A seam of extraction thickness 1.5 m is worked by caving with a subsidence factor of 0.45. The maximum surface subsidence is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Maximum subsidence $S_{max}=a\times m$ (subsidence factor $\times$ extracted thickness).

**Working.** $0.45\times1.5=0.68$ m.

**Answer.** $0.68$ m.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine pump delivers 0.1 m³/s of water against a head of 80 m at 75% efficiency. The input power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=\dfrac{\rho g Q H}{\eta}$ with $\rho=1000$ kg/m³, $g=9.81$ m/s².

**Working.** $P=\dfrac{1000\times9.81\times0.1\times80}{0.75}\div1000=104.64$ kW.

**Answer.** $104.64$ kW.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A DC mine pump motor draws 1980 W from a 220 V supply. The line current is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Power $P=V\,I\Rightarrow I=\dfrac{P}{V}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1980}{220}=9$ A.

**Answer.** $9$ A.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A 'misfire' in blasting refers to:
options: A charge that fails to detonate as intended
An early detonation by one second
questions.solution: **Answer.** A misfire is an unexploded/partly exploded charge needing strict statutory handling.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.15 m³/min into a roadway carrying 40 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.15}{40}\times100=0.38\%$.

**Answer.** $0.38\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.2 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 25 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=0.2\times 25^2=125$ Pa.

**Answer.** $125$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An opencast blast block has burden 7 m, spacing 5 m, bench height 11 m and rock density 2.6 t/m³. If 192.5 kg of explosive is used, the powder factor (tonnes broken per kg) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** PF $=\dfrac{\text{rock broken (t)}}{\text{explosive (kg)}}$.

**Working.** Volume $=7\times5\times11=385$ m³; mass $=385\times2.6=1001$ t; PF $=\dfrac{1001}{192.5}=5.2$ t/kg.

**Answer.** $5.2$ t/kg.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A dump truck of 100 t payload completes a 30-minute haul cycle over a 480-minute working shift. Its production is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trips $=\dfrac{\text{shift}}{\text{cycle}}$; output $=$ trips $\times$ payload.

**Working.** Trips $=\dfrac{480}{30}=16$; output $=16\times100=1600$ t.

**Answer.** $1600$ t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A seam of extraction thickness 2.5 m is worked by caving with a subsidence factor of 0.6. The maximum surface subsidence is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Maximum subsidence $S_{max}=a\times m$ (subsidence factor $\times$ extracted thickness).

**Working.** $0.6\times2.5=1.5$ m.

**Answer.** $1.5$ m.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A dump truck of 60 t payload completes a 18-minute haul cycle over a 450-minute working shift. Its production is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trips $=\dfrac{\text{shift}}{\text{cycle}}$; output $=$ trips $\times$ payload.

**Working.** Trips $=\dfrac{450}{18}=25$; output $=25\times60=1500$ t.

**Answer.** $1500$ t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A jackhammer drills at 0.5 m/min for 28 minutes of net drilling time. The total hole length advanced is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Advance $=$ penetration rate $\times$ time.

**Working.** $0.5\times28=14$ m.

**Answer.** $14$ m.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.15 m³/min into a roadway carrying 15 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.15}{15}\times100=1\%$.

**Answer.** $1\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.15 m³/min into a roadway carrying 50 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.15}{50}\times100=0.3\%$.

**Answer.** $0.3\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.15 m³/min into a roadway carrying 25 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.15}{25}\times100=0.6\%$.

**Answer.** $0.6\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Highwall mining recovers coal from:
options: The exposed seam in the final highwall of an opencast pit
questions.solution: **Answer.** Highwall mining drives unmanned entries into the seam exposed in the opencast highwall.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A concentrator treats 2000 t of ore assaying 1.5% metal at 85% recovery. The mass of metal recovered is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Metal recovered $=$ feed $\times\dfrac{\text{grade}}{100}\times\dfrac{\text{recovery}}{100}$.

**Working.** $2000\times0.015\times0.85=25.5$ t.

**Answer.** $25.5$ t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: In a coal mine, the official who supervises a number of districts and is immediately above the Mining Sirdar is the:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The Overman supervises several districts/sirdars and reports to the Assistant Manager/Manager.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A deposit holds 20 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 2.5 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{20}{2.5}=8$ years.

**Answer.** $8$ years.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 10 m and height 2.5 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=4$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times4)=11.66$ MPa.

**Answer.** $11.66$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A DC mine pump motor draws 550 W from a 110 V supply. The line current is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Power $P=V\,I\Rightarrow I=\dfrac{P}{V}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{550}{110}=5$ A.

**Answer.** $5$ A.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A main fan develops a pressure of 2000 Pa while passing 40 m³/s of air. The air power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=\dfrac{P\times Q}{1000}$ kW ($P$ in Pa, $Q$ in m³/s).

**Working.** $\dfrac{2000\times40}{1000}=80$ kW.

**Answer.** $80$ kW.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine pump delivers 0.04 m³/s of water against a head of 200 m at 70% efficiency. The input power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=\dfrac{\rho g Q H}{\eta}$ with $\rho=1000$ kg/m³, $g=9.81$ m/s².

**Working.** $P=\dfrac{1000\times9.81\times0.04\times200}{0.7}\div1000=112.11$ kW.

**Answer.** $112.11$ kW.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: 'Firedamp' in coal mines refers mainly to:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Firedamp is predominantly methane (CH₄), explosive in the 5–15% range.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 10 m² at a mean velocity of 2 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=2\times 10=20$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $20$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Cut-and-fill stoping is most appropriate when:
options: The orebody is a thin coal seam
Ore is valuable and wall rock is weak
The deposit is flat and shallow
questions.solution: **Answer.** Cut-and-fill suits high-value ore in weak ground, where fill provides support and a working floor.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: 'Convergence' measured in an underground opening refers to:
options: Closure between the roof and the floor
questions.solution: **Answer.** Convergence is the reduction in roof-to-floor (or rib-to-rib) distance over time.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: During pillar extraction, supports are withdrawn using:
options: A remote/mechanical withdrawal device from a place of safety
questions.solution: **Answer.** Supports in the goaf edge are withdrawn remotely so the operator stays in a supported, safe position.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.5 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 25 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=0.5\times 25^2=312.5$ Pa.

**Answer.** $312.5$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.8 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 30 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=0.8\times 30^2=720$ Pa.

**Answer.** $720$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A continuous miner is mainly used in:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Continuous miners cut and load coal in bord-and-pillar (and some pillar extraction) operations.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A stope yields 200 t of ore mixed with 30 t of waste. The percentage dilution is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dilution $=\dfrac{\text{waste}}{\text{ore}+\text{waste}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{30}{230}\times100=13.04\%$.

**Answer.** $13.04\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A jackhammer drills at 0.6 m/min for 25 minutes of net drilling time. The total hole length advanced is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Advance $=$ penetration rate $\times$ time.

**Working.** $0.6\times25=15$ m.

**Answer.** $15$ m.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A belt conveyor carries a uniform load of 35 kg per metre of belt at a speed of 2.2 m/s. Its capacity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Capacity $=3.6\times(\text{kg/m})\times v\,(\text{m/s})$ t/h.

**Working.** $3.6\times35\times2.2=277.2$ t/h.

**Answer.** $277.2$ t/h.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A dump truck of 35 t payload completes a 15-minute haul cycle over a 420-minute working shift. Its production is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trips $=\dfrac{\text{shift}}{\text{cycle}}$; output $=$ trips $\times$ payload.

**Working.** Trips $=\dfrac{420}{15}=28$; output $=28\times35=980$ t.

**Answer.** $980$ t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A stope yields 160 t of ore mixed with 40 t of waste. The percentage dilution is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dilution $=\dfrac{\text{waste}}{\text{ore}+\text{waste}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{40}{200}\times100=20\%$.

**Answer.** $20\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.8 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 40 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=0.8\times 40^2=1280$ Pa.

**Answer.** $1280$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A dump truck of 50 t payload completes a 20-minute haul cycle over a 480-minute working shift. Its production is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trips $=\dfrac{\text{shift}}{\text{cycle}}$; output $=$ trips $\times$ payload.

**Working.** Trips $=\dfrac{480}{20}=24$; output $=24\times50=1200$ t.

**Answer.** $1200$ t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 4 m² at a mean velocity of 1.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=1.5\times 4=6$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $6$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 2 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 10 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=2\times 10^2=200$ Pa.

**Answer.** $200$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A deposit holds 30 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 2 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{30}{2}=15$ years.

**Answer.** $15$ years.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An opencast mine removes 480000 m³ of overburden to win 120000 t of ore. The stripping ratio (m³/t) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Stripping ratio $=\dfrac{\text{overburden}}{\text{ore}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{480000}{120000}=4$ m³/t.

**Answer.** $4$ m³/t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Which of the following coals has the highest fixed-carbon content and calorific value?
questions.solution: **Answer.** Coal rank rises peat → lignite → bituminous → anthracite; anthracite has the highest carbon.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A blast detonates 100 kg of explosive per delay and a structure stands 120 m away. The scaled distance $\left(D/\sqrt{W}\right)$ is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Scaled distance $SD=\dfrac{D}{\sqrt{W}}$ governs peak particle velocity in blast-vibration control.

**Working.** $\dfrac{120}{\sqrt{100}}=\dfrac{120}{10}=12$.

**Answer.** $12$ m/kg$^{1/2}$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Wet drilling is preferred over dry drilling primarily to:
options: Suppress respirable dust at the source
questions.solution: **Answer.** Water flushing suppresses silica/coal dust, reducing pneumoconiosis risk.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 8 m² at a mean velocity of 3.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=3.5\times 8=28$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $28$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Acid mine drainage (AMD) is produced mainly by the oxidation of:
options: Pyrite (iron sulphide) in the presence of water and air
questions.solution: **Answer.** Pyrite (FeS₂) oxidises to sulphuric acid and iron salts, lowering water pH.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 18 m² at a mean velocity of 1.8 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=1.8\times 18=32.4$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $32.4$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 2 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 30 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=2\times 30^2=1800$ Pa.

**Answer.** $1800$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Which Indian state is the leading producer of gypsum, used in cement and plaster?
questions.solution: **Answer.** Rajasthan (Bikaner, Nagaur, Jaisalmer) leads India's gypsum production.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Transferring surface coordinates and bearing down a vertical shaft to underground workings is called:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Correlation (shaft plumbing) carries surface orientation and position underground.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: The gradient most favourable for self-acting (gravity) haulage of loaded tubs is one that:
options: Falls in the direction of the loaded run
questions.solution: **Answer.** In self-acting haulage the loaded tubs descend, hauling the empties up via gravity.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A device that lets intake and return airways cross without mixing is a(n):
questions.solution: **Answer.** An air crossing (overcast) carries one airstream over another, keeping intake and return separate.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A fan characteristic is $P=18-0.6Q^2$ and the mine resistance is $P=0.5\,Q^2$. The operating air quantity $Q$ (intersection) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Operating point: fan pressure = mine pressure.

**Working.** $18-0.6Q^2=0.5Q^2\Rightarrow Q^2=\dfrac{18}{1.1}\Rightarrow Q=4.05$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $4.05$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 2 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 25 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=2\times 25^2=1250$ Pa.

**Answer.** $1250$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A DC mine pump motor draws 2750 W from a 550 V supply. The line current is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Power $P=V\,I\Rightarrow I=\dfrac{P}{V}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{2750}{550}=5$ A.

**Answer.** $5$ A.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.2 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 10 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=0.2\times 10^2=20$ Pa.

**Answer.** $20$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Two ore blocks assay: 150 t at 0.9% and 350 t at 1.8%. The combined (tonnage-weighted) grade is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Weighted grade $=\dfrac{\sum(t_i g_i)}{\sum t_i}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{150\times0.9+350\times1.8}{500}=1.53\%$.

**Answer.** $1.53\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Under the Mines Act, 1952, no person shall be employed BELOW ground for more than ___ in any day:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Section 30(b) limits below-ground work to 8 hours per day.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An opencast blast block has burden 4 m, spacing 3 m, bench height 6 m and rock density 2.7 t/m³. If 28.8 kg of explosive is used, the powder factor (tonnes broken per kg) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** PF $=\dfrac{\text{rock broken (t)}}{\text{explosive (kg)}}$.

**Working.** Volume $=4\times3\times6=72$ m³; mass $=72\times2.7=194.4$ t; PF $=\dfrac{194.4}{28.8}=6.75$ t/kg.

**Answer.** $6.75$ t/kg.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Statutory mine survey plans must be kept up to date and corrected at intervals not exceeding:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Survey plans must be brought up to date at least once every three months.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Two ore blocks assay: 400 t at 2% and 200 t at 3.5%. The combined (tonnage-weighted) grade is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Weighted grade $=\dfrac{\sum(t_i g_i)}{\sum t_i}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{400\times2+200\times3.5}{600}=2.5\%$.

**Answer.** $2.5\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.5 m³/min into a roadway carrying 30 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.5}{30}\times100=1.67\%$.

**Answer.** $1.67\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: The void left in the strata after extraction of coal (e.g. behind a longwall face) is the:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The de-stressed, caved void behind the face is the goaf or gob.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.2 m³/min into a roadway carrying 50 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.2}{50}\times100=0.4\%$.

**Answer.** $0.4\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: The Sanu deposit in Jaisalmer district, Rajasthan, is an important source of:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Sanu limestone (Jaisalmer) supplies flux-grade limestone to steel plants.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: In metalliferous mines, the statutory first-line supervisor equivalent to the coal-mine Sirdar is the:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The Mining Mate is the first-line supervisor under the MMR 1961.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A loaded train of mass 12000 kg is hauled up a gradient of 1 in 40. With a tractive (rolling) resistance coefficient of 0.03, the rope pull is approximately _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rope pull $T=Wg\left(\frac{1}{n}+\mu\right)$ (gravity + resistance).

**Working.** $T=12000\times9.81\times(\frac{1}{40}+0.03)=6474.6$ N.

**Answer.** $6474.6$ N.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A main fan develops a pressure of 3000 Pa while passing 25 m³/s of air. The air power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=\dfrac{P\times Q}{1000}$ kW ($P$ in Pa, $Q$ in m³/s).

**Working.** $\dfrac{3000\times25}{1000}=75$ kW.

**Answer.** $75$ kW.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.7 m³/min into a roadway carrying 20 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.7}{20}\times100=3.5\%$.

**Answer.** $3.5\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An opencast mine removes 320000 m³ of overburden to win 80000 t of ore. The stripping ratio (m³/t) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Stripping ratio $=\dfrac{\text{overburden}}{\text{ore}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{320000}{80000}=4$ m³/t.

**Answer.** $4$ m³/t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 10 m² at a mean velocity of 1.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=1.5\times 10=15$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $15$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.7 m³/min into a roadway carrying 50 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.7}{50}\times100=1.4\%$.

**Answer.** $1.4\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A fan characteristic is $P=24-0.35Q^2$ and the mine resistance is $P=0.5\,Q^2$. The operating air quantity $Q$ (intersection) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Operating point: fan pressure = mine pressure.

**Working.** $24-0.35Q^2=0.5Q^2\Rightarrow Q^2=\dfrac{24}{0.85}\Rightarrow Q=5.31$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $5.31$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: In shaft winding, a 'skip' is used mainly to hoist:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Skips hoist loose mineral; cages carry men, materials and tubs.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.7 m³/min into a roadway carrying 25 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.7}{25}\times100=2.8\%$.

**Answer.** $2.8\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: In bord-and-pillar development with square pillars of side 20 m and galleries 4 m wide, the percentage of coal extracted on development is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For square pillars (centre spacing $a+w$), development extraction $=\left(1-\dfrac{a^2}{(a+w)^2}\right)\times100$.

**Working.** $\left(1-\dfrac{20^2}{24^2}\right)\times100=30.56\%$.

**Answer.** $30.56\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Under the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017, gassy seams are classified into how many degrees?
questions.solution: **Answer.** CMR 2017 classifies gassy seams as first, second and third degree.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An 'adit' is best described as a:
options: Near-horizontal entry driven into a hillside to reach a deposit
questions.solution: **Answer.** An adit is a horizontal/near-horizontal drift driven from a hillside, often self-draining.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A stope yields 100 t of ore mixed with 10 t of waste. The percentage dilution is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dilution $=\dfrac{\text{waste}}{\text{ore}+\text{waste}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{10}{110}\times100=9.09\%$.

**Answer.** $9.09\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: ANFO, a common bulk blasting agent, is a mixture of:
questions.solution: **Answer.** ANFO = Ammonium Nitrate (94%) + Fuel Oil (6%); cheap, used in dry opencast holes.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A blast detonates 81 kg of explosive per delay and a structure stands 180 m away. The scaled distance $\left(D/\sqrt{W}\right)$ is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Scaled distance $SD=\dfrac{D}{\sqrt{W}}$ governs peak particle velocity in blast-vibration control.

**Working.** $\dfrac{180}{\sqrt{81}}=\dfrac{180}{9}=20$.

**Answer.** $20$ m/kg$^{1/2}$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: India's principal rock-phosphate deposit is mined at Jhamarkotra in which Rajasthan district?
questions.solution: **Answer.** Jhamarkotra (Udaipur) is India's largest rock-phosphate source, mined by RSMML.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 15 m² at a mean velocity of 1.8 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=1.8\times 15=27$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $27$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.4 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 10 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=0.4\times 10^2=40$ Pa.

**Answer.** $40$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A blast detonates 64 kg of explosive per delay and a structure stands 200 m away. The scaled distance $\left(D/\sqrt{W}\right)$ is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Scaled distance $SD=\dfrac{D}{\sqrt{W}}$ governs peak particle velocity in blast-vibration control.

**Working.** $\dfrac{200}{\sqrt{64}}=\dfrac{200}{8}=25$.

**Answer.** $25$ m/kg$^{1/2}$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 1.5 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 10 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=1.5\times 10^2=150$ Pa.

**Answer.** $150$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.5 m³/min into a roadway carrying 25 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.5}{25}\times100=2\%$.

**Answer.** $2\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Rock Quality Designation (RQD) is computed from drill core as the percentage of:
options: Sum of intact core pieces ≥ 10 cm long to total core run
Voids to solid in the core
questions.solution: **Answer.** RQD = (Σ lengths of sound core pieces ≥10 cm) / (total core run length) × 100.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 15 m² at a mean velocity of 3 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=3\times 15=45$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $45$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 10 m² at a mean velocity of 3 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=3\times 10=30$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $30$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 20 m² at a mean velocity of 1.2 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=1.2\times 20=24$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $24$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: The current National Mineral Policy of India was promulgated in:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The National Mineral Policy, 2019 replaced the NMP 2008.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: The Mines Act, 1952 entitles a worker to at least one ___ as a day of rest:
questions.solution: **Answer.** A weekly day of rest is mandated by the Act (Section 28).

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine pump delivers 0.06 m³/s of water against a head of 100 m at 72% efficiency. The input power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=\dfrac{\rho g Q H}{\eta}$ with $\rho=1000$ kg/m³, $g=9.81$ m/s².

**Working.** $P=\dfrac{1000\times9.81\times0.06\times100}{0.72}\div1000=81.75$ kW.

**Answer.** $81.75$ kW.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 20 m² at a mean velocity of 4 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=4\times 20=80$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $80$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 8 m and height 2 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=4$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times4)=11.66$ MPa.

**Answer.** $11.66$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A DC mine pump motor draws 1100 W from a 110 V supply. The line current is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Power $P=V\,I\Rightarrow I=\dfrac{P}{V}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1100}{110}=10$ A.

**Answer.** $10$ A.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 7 m and height 3.5 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=2$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times2)=8.55$ MPa.

**Answer.** $8.55$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A seam of extraction thickness 3.2 m is worked by caving with a subsidence factor of 0.68. The maximum surface subsidence is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Maximum subsidence $S_{max}=a\times m$ (subsidence factor $\times$ extracted thickness).

**Working.** $0.68\times3.2=2.18$ m.

**Answer.** $2.18$ m.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A dump truck of 55 t payload completes a 20-minute haul cycle over a 360-minute working shift. Its production is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trips $=\dfrac{\text{shift}}{\text{cycle}}$; output $=$ trips $\times$ payload.

**Working.** Trips $=\dfrac{360}{20}=18$; output $=18\times55=990$ t.

**Answer.** $990$ t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.2 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 30 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=0.2\times 30^2=180$ Pa.

**Answer.** $180$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 18 m² at a mean velocity of 2 m/s. The air quantity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity $Q=v\times A$.

**Working.** $Q=2\times 18=36$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $36$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An ore block of 900 m³ has bulk density 3.5 t/m³. Its tonnage is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tonnage $=$ volume $\times$ density.

**Working.** $900\times3.5=3150$ t.

**Answer.** $3150$ t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: The Aravalli range, host to Rajasthan's base-metal deposits, is composed mainly of:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The Aravallis are among the oldest (Precambrian) fold mountains, of metamorphic/igneous rock.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A shovel with a 10 m³ bucket works at a fill factor of 0.9 and a cycle time of 40 s. Its theoretical output is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Output $=\dfrac{\text{bucket}\times\text{fill factor}\times3600}{\text{cycle (s)}}$ m³/h.

**Working.** $\dfrac{10\times0.9\times3600}{40}=810$ m³/h.

**Answer.** $810$ m³/h.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Certificates of Competency (Manager, Overman, Sirdar, Surveyor) are granted by the:
options: Board of Mining Examinations under DGMS
questions.solution: **Answer.** The Board of Mining Examinations, under the Director General of Mines Safety, conducts competency examinations and grants certificates.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Stone dusting in coal mine roadways is done to:
options: Render coal dust incapable of propagating an explosion
questions.solution: **Answer.** Inert stone dust raises the incombustible content so coal dust cannot sustain a flame front.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Backfilling and revegetating mined-out opencast land is termed:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Reclamation restores mined land to a usable, stable, vegetated condition.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A belt conveyor carries a uniform load of 50 kg per metre of belt at a speed of 2.5 m/s. Its capacity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Capacity $=3.6\times(\text{kg/m})\times v\,(\text{m/s})$ t/h.

**Working.** $3.6\times50\times2.5=450$ t/h.

**Answer.** $450$ t/h.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.4 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 40 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=R\,Q^2$ (Atkinson square law).

**Working.** $P=0.4\times 40^2=640$ Pa.

**Answer.** $640$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A blast detonates 36 kg of explosive per delay and a structure stands 150 m away. The scaled distance $\left(D/\sqrt{W}\right)$ is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Scaled distance $SD=\dfrac{D}{\sqrt{W}}$ governs peak particle velocity in blast-vibration control.

**Working.** $\dfrac{150}{\sqrt{36}}=\dfrac{150}{6}=25$.

**Answer.** $25$ m/kg$^{1/2}$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A stope yields 150 t of ore mixed with 15 t of waste. The percentage dilution is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dilution $=\dfrac{\text{waste}}{\text{ore}+\text{waste}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{15}{165}\times100=9.09\%$.

**Answer.** $9.09\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A jackhammer drills at 0.5 m/min for 40 minutes of net drilling time. The total hole length advanced is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Advance $=$ penetration rate $\times$ time.

**Working.** $0.5\times40=20$ m.

**Answer.** $20$ m.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.5 m³/min into a roadway carrying 20 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.5}{20}\times100=2.5\%$.

**Answer.** $2.5\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A concentrator treats 1200 t of ore assaying 2% metal at 80% recovery. The mass of metal recovered is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Metal recovered $=$ feed $\times\dfrac{\text{grade}}{100}\times\dfrac{\text{recovery}}{100}$.

**Working.** $1200\times0.02\times0.8=19.2$ t.

**Answer.** $19.2$ t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A belt conveyor carries a uniform load of 45 kg per metre of belt at a speed of 1.6 m/s. Its capacity is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Capacity $=3.6\times(\text{kg/m})\times v\,(\text{m/s})$ t/h.

**Working.** $3.6\times45\times1.6=259.2$ t/h.

**Answer.** $259.2$ t/h.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Enforcement of the Mines Act, 1952 and the regulations made under it is the responsibility of the:
questions.solution: **Answer.** DGMS, under the Ministry of Labour and Employment, enforces the Mines Act and regulations.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A loaded train of mass 5000 kg is hauled up a gradient of 1 in 20. With a tractive (rolling) resistance coefficient of 0.02, the rope pull is approximately _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rope pull $T=Wg\left(\frac{1}{n}+\mu\right)$ (gravity + resistance).

**Working.** $T=5000\times9.81\times(\frac{1}{20}+0.02)=3433.5$ N.

**Answer.** $3433.5$ N.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.2 m³/min into a roadway carrying 30 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** % gas $=\dfrac{\text{gas flow}}{\text{air flow}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{0.2}{30}\times100=0.67\%$.

**Answer.** $0.67\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An ore block of 1600 m³ has bulk density 2.75 t/m³. Its tonnage is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tonnage $=$ volume $\times$ density.

**Working.** $1600\times2.75=4400$ t.

**Answer.** $4400$ t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An opencast blast block has burden 5 m, spacing 5 m, bench height 9 m and rock density 2.6 t/m³. If 112.5 kg of explosive is used, the powder factor (tonnes broken per kg) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** PF $=\dfrac{\text{rock broken (t)}}{\text{explosive (kg)}}$.

**Working.** Volume $=5\times5\times9=225$ m³; mass $=225\times2.6=585$ t; PF $=\dfrac{585}{112.5}=5.2$ t/kg.

**Answer.** $5.2$ t/kg.
